mnemonics: PRIVACY is a Human Right: Protect Resources, Implement Controls, Verify Access, Act on Threats, Classify Data, You are responsible.
keyPoints: Focus on understanding the principles of data classification (Public, Internal, Confidential, Restricted).
Learn the key differences between data security (protecting data) and data privacy (governing data use).
Review the official resources listed; they are primary sources for best practices and legal requirements.
commonMistakes: Skipping the suggested official resources (e.g., NIST frameworks, GDPR guidelines) and relying only on module text.
Confusing data encryption (a security control) with data anonymization (a privacy technique).
Memorizing controls without understanding the 'why' behind them for different data classification levels.
